## Today's Priorities

1. **HeartPathBloom — Day 6. Send a direct follow-up now.**
   The service agreement has been live at `heartpathbloom-agreement.netlify.app` since April 20. Six days and still no signed agreement, no $1,750 deposit, no confirmed contact from Tisa or Kendalyn. This is your biggest open revenue item — $3,500 setup + $500/month retainer, and zero work can start without the signature and deposit. Check `thurrenterprise@gmail.com` first. If nothing is there, send a warm, personal message today — reference the agreement URL, ask if they have questions, and make it easy to take the next step. Don't let this go cold over a weekend.

2. **Manual N8N check — browser required.**
   The sandbox proxy has blocked health checks every night since April 20 — six consecutive nights unverifiable. This is an environment limitation, not a confirmed outage, but you haven't had eyes on the cloud instance in nearly a week. Open `https://therrancecarrothers.app.n8n.cloud` in your browser today and confirm the instance is up. Verify that `Thurnos Prime — Conversational Bot` and `My workflow` both show `active: true`. You can also run `bash ~/Documents/ThurrSolutions/Thurnos/commands/check_n8n.sh` from Terminal.

3. **Resolve the 4 empty client folders.**
   5StarHospice, Andy Life Insurance, Christy Nail Business, and Chic-Fil-A are all empty shell folders — no notes, no status, no context. If any are warm or active leads, create a `Notes/status.md` with current standing. If they're dead, remove the folders. These blind spots make briefings incomplete and clutter the pipeline view.
